item.content:encoded:
Key Takeaways

  • Microsoft plans to release a perpetual license version of Office 2024, catering to those who prefer to purchase the product once and have it forever.
  • The Office 2024 preview can be accessed through the "DevMain" channel, which is intended for Microsoft's staff to test before customer availability.
  • It is recommended to use a virtual machine or a secondary PC and follow specific instructions to avoid potential issues with existing Office licenses when installing the preview version.

The subscription-focused licensing model isn't everyone's cup of tea, particularly for many businesses. Microsoft seems to have gotten the memo, because we can now confirm that the Redmond giant plans to release a fresh new perpetual license version of its productivity application suite next year in the form of Office 2024.

The Microsoft 365 lineup (formerly known as Office 365) caters to both regular consumers and enterprise customers, but it is fundamentally different from a traditional feature-freeze release. There is absolutely no doubt that a subscription-based product like Microsoft 365 is a seamless way to let users access its always up-to-date suite of tools and services, but there exist use cases where people and organizations actually prefer a perpetual license for Office that allows them to purchase a product once and to have it forever.

Some even speculated that Office 2021 was to be the last perpetual license version of the product, but a new version is definitely on the way. We first discovered the existence of the branding and the corresponding licensing data in an internal preview build of Microsoft Office through the Click-to-Run (C2R) release API, but eventually managed to fetch and install the version to get an early glimpse of Office 2024. We'll likely learn more about it at Microsoft Ignite, which will take place next week in Seattle.

For now, only the Windows edition of the Microsoft Office suite has received the bump in the version number - that too for the Long Term Servicing Channel (LTSC). With that said, judging from the lifecycle policy of the equivalent Mac variant, both are apparently scheduled to get a new coat of paint in the near future via public preview channels. In a nutshell, if you're looking for the next one-time-purchase Office build for your own home computer or your Mac, look to the third/fourth quarter of 2024.

If you want to give the work-in-progress build of Office 2024 a shot, you can easily do so by following our guide in the next section. Nonetheless, always create a full backup of your PC before such tinkering jobs, so that you can at least restore a working instance in case things go south.

How to install the Microsoft Office 2024 preview right now

For the unaware, Microsoft maintains a plethora of update channels for different Office SKUs and editions. The main goal of this design is to offer as much as flexibility in the ability to choose between different deployment variables (e.g. the availability of new feature updates or support duration). In this particular scenario, we are mostly interested in the "DevMain" channel, cause one can now grab the Office 2024 preview licenses from there.

The DevMain branch is a classic example of eating your own dog food, due to the fact that this semi-public update channel is intended for Microsoft's own staff to test it before it is made available to customers. Fortunately, you don't need any special credentials or such to fetch the builds, but it is definitely not recommended for your daily driver.

Before we continue further, keep in mind that messing with Office update channels is not for the faint of heart. You could completely mess up your existing Microsoft Office license, or lose it altogether if you don't know what you're doing. We suggest using a virtual machine or a secondary PC for these kinds of experiments.

We are going to use two awesome open-source projects by a developer named abbodi1406 for downloading as well as the installation of the Office binaries. The first one is called "Yet Another Office Click To Run URL Generator" (or YAOCTRU for short), while the second tool is known as "Yet Another Office Click To Run Installer" (aka YAOCTRI). You can read more about them in their GitHub repository.

  1. Download the latest version of YAOCTRU zip archive from the author's GitHub scripts repo.
    • At the time of writing, the latest version of YAOCTRU is v9.0.

  2. Extract the contents of the archive, then execute the file named YAOCTRU_Generator.cmd. You don't need admin privileges, so a regular double click would do just fine.
  3. Opt for the DevMain Channel entry in the update channel selection menu. Then choose your preferred architecture of the binaries (32-bit/64-bit), language, and Office source components.
  4. At this stage, you need to select the suitable output format. The tool can generate aria2, wget, and curl-compatible download scripts, and you can even settle with an old-school text file with URLs for manual downloading using your favorite download manager.
  5. Download the preview version of Office 2024 using the script/URL list you generated earlier.
    • Note that Windows 11 and Windows 10 version 1803 or later ship with a curl executable out of the box. As a result, you can use the curl download script as it is without the need to install anything else.

  6. Now that the source files are ready, download the latest version of YAOCTRI zip archive from the author's GitHub scripts repo for the installation part.
    • At the time of writing, the latest version of YAOCTRI is v10.7.

  7. Extract the contents of the archive, and move Reference-C2R_Config.ini, YAOCTRI_Configurator.cmd, and YAOCTRI_Installer.cmd inside the folder where you downloaded the Office components.
    • This part is necessary because the only volume channel has been updated with the 2024 branding. The remaining files in the package (excluding the readme) can be used to install the retail editions of Office, which is of no use in this case.

  8. Right-click on the YAOCTRI_Configurator.cmd file and run it as administrator. It should automatically detect the Office installation files.
  9. At the product selection prompt, make sure to choose one of the 2024 branded options. For instance, the Office ProPlus 2024 entry will cover most of the common Office programs.
  10. If everything goes right, the script will help you to finalize the deployment configuration data and install the suite. Choose Install Now to start the wizard.
  11. After Office is successfully installed, open any Office app, then go to File => Account to see the Office 2024 branding.

Currently, the 2024 bits are more or less the same as the LTSC 2021 release. Of course, Microsoft has started backporting some cool new features from the upstream channels, but it is way too early to spot anything significant.

Due to the fact that we installed the preview version of Office 2024 using the volume license channel, you can't use your retail Office key to activate the product. It's the Key Management Services (KMS) client key, which is used internally, so that you can have a short grace period to play around.

In case you want to revert back and reinstall the retail/volume build of Office you had earlier, you should use the Office Scrubber project from the same developer. Just download the latest version of the script (v11 at the time of writing), execute it, reboot, initiate the installer of the stable channel release of Microsoft Office, and you're good to go.

item.title: Score this 240W USB-C 2-pack cable bundle for just $8 in this limited-time deal
item.itunes:title: Score this 240W USB-C 2-pack cable bundle for just $8 in this limited-time deal
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
LISEN 240W USB-C cable
Use code "20D5CUGV" to save more
$8 $16 Save $8

An excellent 2-pack bundle of USB-C cables with charging speeds that max out at 240W. You can now grab these cables for just $8 for a limited time.

$8 at Amazon

There are a lot of great USB cables out there right now, but if you're looking to optimize the charging speed of your products, you're going to need a powerful PD charger and these Lisen 240W USB-C charging cables. While higher-end cables can cost quite a bit, these cables are now on sale for a great price that knocks them down to just $10. Furthermore, by using the coupon code "20D5CUGV" you can save an additional $2 off the discounted price, bringing it down to just $8, or $4 a piece, for a limited time.

As far as what you can expect with these Lisen USB-C cables? Well, first and foremost, you're going to be able to experience extreme charging with speeds up to 240W. The cables measure in at 6.6 feet and offer excellent durability thanks to the cable's braided exterior and extensive testing that the brand states will allow the cables to withstand up to 48,000 bends. If that wasn't enough, the company also provides a 12-month warranty on this product with 24-hour support, just in case something goes wrong.

For the most part, you're getting really good cables at an extremely affordable price. While they're perfect for laptops and tablets, you can also use these cables for smartphones too. Data transfers are also possible, but you'll be maxing out at 480Mbps. So if you're looking move a lot of data quickly, there are going to be better options. WIth that said, you're still getting a great deal here, and it's well worth the money to pick these up while you still can.

item.title: Apple can't seem to innovate anymore
item.itunes:title: Apple can't seem to innovate anymore
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Despite Apple's reputation for innovation, recent hardware upgrades have been incremental, lacking the major changes that would truly qualify as innovation.
  • The iPhone 15 Pro Max, for example, highlights how Apple is catching up to features that already exist in other devices, rather than introducing groundbreaking ideas.
  • Calling the M3 Macs innovative is questionable, as they are essentially just spec updates without any significant creative changes. The word "innovation" is losing its meaning in Apple's keynotes.

From popular iPhones, to versatile iPads and unrivaled Macs, Apple's product line features some of the best consumer technology released to date. And what makes these devices even more powerful is the tightly integrated ecosystem that ties them all together. Many customers, including myself, started with just an iPhone, only to find themselves with a full suite of Apple devices within a few months or years of use. Though, despite these devices' objective excellence, their hardware upgrades lately have been pretty incremental.

Incremental upgrades in both the hardware and software departments are completely understandable at this point. Apple has mastered its devices' design, performance, and operating systems, so there's not much to ask for. My issue, however, is with the company throwing around the word "innovation" during its keynotes, when its recent products have been lacking that aspect.

iPhone 15 Pro Max

The iPhone 15 Pro Max's 6.7-inch OLED display 

I upgraded from an iPhone 14 Pro to an iPhone 15 Pro Max to see what 2023's Apple innovation in the mobile department was all about. The main differences between the two phones are the Action button, improved camera, USB-C port, and titanium build. Starting with the Action button, other OEMs have included something similar on their devices for many years now. Replacing a mute switch by a clickable button (that is limited to a single action, by the way) is a welcome tweak, not an "innovation." Innovation in this sense is a major change driven by creativity and that unlocks new potentials. A recycled, half-baked idea is not it.

Similarly, introducing an improved zoom when other manufacturers have been beating that for years is just catching up. It's not the mind-blowing kind of innovation that we witnessed when the original MacBook Air or Face ID debuted, for example. The same can be said about USB-C and the titanium design. They are all welcome changes, but they lack the unique creativity that helped Apple stand out in the past.

Again, I'm aware that flagship smartphones have matured, and I'm not requesting groundbreaking changes on a yearly basis. I'm simply pointing out that the word "innovation" is losing its meaning because of how redundantly Apple has been throwing it around in its keynotes.

Apple Watch Series 9

Another example revolves around the latest Apple Watches. Arguably the biggest highlight of the Apple Watch Ultra 2 and Series 9 was the double-tap gesture. For those unfamiliar, this offering has existed for years as an accessibility feature. On the latest models, however, Apple relies on the faster S9 processor to supposedly make it more accurate and further integrate its functionality into the system.

This feature, on paper, is pretty innovative, I have to admit. Many of us use their noses to tap the watch's screen when the other hand is occupied. So being able to rely on a two-finger tap instead is certainly a welcome change. Well, except that the feature doesn't work reliably. XDA's Editor-in-Chief, Rich Woods, and I have both tried to actively use this feature on his Ultra 2 and my Series 9, and its implementation is wonky. False triggers often happen throughout the day, and the watch frequently fails to detect intentional input.

The entire argument behind Apple making this feature exclusive to the new models is the improved accuracy. When said accuracy is a no-show, then one of the watch's biggest highlights is invalid. Looking back at its predecessors, most of them have also been incremental upgrades. Despite that, Apple continues to claim it's innovating, when in reality it has been just polishing its devices to provide a more reliable and powerful experience.

M3 Macs

Another recent batch of not-so-innovative products is the M3 Macs. In fact, Apple's "Scary Fast" event is what drove me to writing this piece in the first place. Towards the end of the keynote, Apple CEO Tim Cook repeated the word "innovate" around three times in a row, when the 30-minute Mac launch could've been a press release.

I will state again that I'm not expecting these products to introduce groundbreaking changes with every single upgrade. However, calling them innovative when they're quite literally a spec bump is laughable. The 24-inch iMac, which hadn't been refreshed in over two years, could've used some more creative changes. The company didn't even switch its accessories from Lightning to USB-C. Despite the valid improvements of the M3 chip family, I wouldn't call 2023's iMac and MacBook Pro models an innovation.

The illusory truth effect

The word "innovation" itself could refer to small changes, too. However, Apple used to use it when actually coming up with genius ideas that surpass its rivals' executions. Today, Apple products remain some of the most reliable and powerful devices available to consumers. Nonetheless, the recent models lack the creative twists that would usually highlight Apple's accomplishments.

It's proven that repeating an inaccurate statement over and over again can actually make it seem true to the hearer. As someone who watches Apple's newest keynotes more than once for work reasons, I've noticed how the phrasing plays a role in influencing viewers' opinions. And the more immersed someone is in the company's products and keynotes, the more likely they'll miss these cues, as they just become typical wordings. However, ask an expert who has never watched an Apple keynote or ad in the past to view some of its recent events, and they'll likely question what the so-called "innovation" is all about.

item.title: Best 4TB SSDs
item.itunes:title: Best 4TB SSDs
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
As we create and consume content at an unprecedented pace, the need for substantial storage capacity, coupled with rapid data access, has become paramount. This is where the best 4TB SSDs step into the spotlight, offering a harmonious union of generous storage space and remarkable speed.

We've scoured the market to present you with a unique selection of the best SSDs available in a 4TB capacity today. Whether you're a creative professional who needs to store a huge backlog of assets, an enthusiast craving blazing speeds, or a gamer looking to load your favorite titles in the blink of an eye, our guide has you covered.

  • Source: Sabrent
    Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus 4TB SSD
    Editor's choice
    $350 at Amazon
  • Source: Samsung
    Samsung 990 PRO SSD 4TB
    Best performance
    $280 at Amazon
  • Source: Crucial
    Crucial T700 4TB SSD
    Premium pick
    $480 at Amazon
  • Source: XPG
    XPG GAMMIX S70 BLADE 4TB SSD
    Best value
    $263 at Amazon
  • Source: Corsair
    Corsair MP600 PRO LPX 4TB SSD
    Best SSD for PS5
    $280 at Amazon
  • Source: Teamgroup
    TEAMGROUP MP44 4TB SSD
    Best DRAM-less SSD
    $232 at Amazon
  • Source: Crucial
    Crucial P3 Plus 4TB SSD
    Best budget SSD
    $215 at Amazon
  • Source: Crucial
    Crucial MX500 4TB SSD
    Best SATA SSD
    $216 at Amazon
Source: Sabrent
Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus 4TB SSD
Editor's choice
$350 $1000 Save $650

The Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus is our Editor's Choice for the best 4 TB SSD, known for its exceptional performance, high reliability, and stylish design. However, its premium pricing might not suit budget-conscious buyers.

Pros
  • Excellent sequential performance
  • Solid long-term reliability
  • Even has an 8TB option
Cons
  • Relatively higher price
$350 at Amazon

The Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus earns our Editor's Choice for the best 4 TB SSD, setting the bar high thanks to its PCIe 4.0 storage technology. Its exceptional performance, reliability, and respectable value make it an ideal choice, particularly for premium gaming PCs.

Performance is where the Rocket 4 Plus truly shines. With a blazing sequential read speed of 7000 MB/s and a write speed of 6850 MB/s, it maxes out the capabilities of the PCIe 4.0 protocol. The SSD features a PCIe Gen 4×4 interface, a Phison PS50180-E18 flash controller, and an external DDR4 cache, improving its sustained performance. The 3D TLC NAND provides a perfect balance of speed and reliability.

On the topic of reliability, the 4TB Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus impresses here with a 3000 TBW rating, making it one of the most durable premium SSDs available. The use of Micron B27B TLC NAND Flash, along with advanced wear leveling and error correction code features, further solidifies its dependability.

Sabrent's attention to design is evident with the sleek rose-gold aesthetic, setting it apart from other SSDs. The optional massive heatsink not only adds to its visual appeal but also ensures optimal thermal performance should the user choose that option.

The Crucial P3 Plus stands out as the best budget SSD, offering a respectable price-to-performance ratio at a very modest price point.

Performance is a strong suit of the Crucial P3 Plus, providing PCIe Gen 4.0 x4 speeds at a budget-friendly price point.

It utilizes Micron's 176-layer QLC NAND and the Phison PS5021-E2IT controller, resulting in respectable sequential read and write speeds of up to 4800 MB/s and 4100 MB/s, respectively. These speeds are competitive and suitable for a wide range of applications, including gaming and everyday tasks.

In terms of reliability, the 800TBW (TeraBytes Written) rating may not be the highest in its class, but Crucial's choice of high-quality NAND flash and a 5-year warranty offers peace of mind, ensuring the drive's longevity and dependability for several years to come.

The design of the Crucial P3 Plus is minimalist and straightforward, in line with its budget-oriented nature. The Crucial P3 Plus really stands out from the crowd when you consider its value proposition. It offers capacities up to 4TB, all at a lower price point compared to other PCIe Gen 4.0 drives. While the TBW rating is not the highest, it remains a cost-effective SSD solution for individuals on a tight budget, offering a balance of performance and affordability.

Source: Crucial
Crucial MX500 4TB SSD
Best SATA SSD
$216 $220 Save $4

The Crucial MX500 is a highly economical SATA SSD that offers good performance, durability, and a range of capacities, making it ideal for gamers who are looking to increase the storage capacity of their PCs. While it may not match NVMe speeds, it excels within its SATA limitations.

Pros
  • Very economical pricing
  • Great for mass storage
  • Various available capacities
Cons
  • Lower speeds than NVMe
$216 at Amazon

The Crucial MX500 excels within the limitations of its SATA 6Gbps interface. While it may not match the speeds of NVMe drives, it remains one of the fastest SATA SSDs available. With sequential read speeds of 560 MB/s and write speeds of 510 MB/s on the 4TB model, it delivers acceptable performance. Its random read/write rating of 95K/90K IOPS ensures sustained responsiveness, making it ideal for the mass storage of games and large files in gaming PCs.

The Crucial MX500 is also quite reliable, boasting an endurance rating of 1000 TBW for the 4TB model. This is an impressive figure for a SATA SSD, ensuring its durability and longevity, particularly given its competitive price point. The use of 3D TLC NAND strikes a respectable balance between performance and durability, making it a dependable long-term choice.

The design of the Crucial MX500 in its 2.5-inch form factor is visually appealing, with a silver body and blue accents that add a touch of style to the drive. This design allows users to showcase the drive in their setups, enhancing its overall aesthetic appeal.

The MX500 runs away with the prize when it comes to the value discussion. SATA SSDs are known for their affordability, and the MX500 delivers superb value, offering an excellent price per gigabyte ratio. It is an ideal choice for gamers looking for cost-effective mass storage for games and large files in their gaming PCs. Combining a larger SATA drive like the MX500 with a smaller NVMe drive can allow gamers to achieve the best of both worlds without breaking the bank.

Choosing the best SSD for your needs

Selecting the best SSD for your particular needs can be a bit of a challenge. There are so many factors that can sway your decision one way or another depending on your preferences. However, if you want the best SSD for gaming, we feel pretty confident with our Editor’s Choice, the Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus.

The Rocket 4 Plus delivers exceptional overall performance, coupled with solid long-term reliability, and a stylish design. However, If you prioritize high performance and are willing to invest a bit more, the Samsung 990 PRO is also a compelling choice. You can even consider the blistering fast Corsair T700 PCIe 5.0 M.2 drive if you are feeling a bit brave.

Source: Sabrent
Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus 4TB SSD
$350 $1000 Save $650

The Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus, with its exceptional PCIe 4.0 technology, outstanding performance, and impressive 4TB capacity, is the top choice for premium gaming PCs.

$350 at Amazon

On the other end of the spectrum, we particularly like the XPG GAMMIX S70 BLADE, as it is certainly one of the best M.2 SSDs on a budget. The Crucial MX500 is a great wildcard pick for those gamers who just want to increase the storage capacity of their gaming rigs without breaking the bank.

Although it is easy to get confused when building a new PC for the first time, it is hard to go wrong with your SSD choice if you go with these curated picks. Choosing an SSD has become easier than ever thanks to the wealth of great options found on the market.

The Crucial MX500 excels within the limitations of its SATA 6Gbps interface. While it may not match the speeds of NVMe drives, it remains one of the fastest SATA SSDs available. With sequential read speeds of 560 MB/s and write speeds of 510 MB/s on the 4TB model, it delivers acceptable performance. Its random read/write rating of 95K/90K IOPS ensures sustained responsiveness, making it ideal for the mass storage of games and large files in gaming PCs.

The Crucial MX500 is also quite reliable, boasting an endurance rating of 1000 TBW for the 4TB model. This is an impressive figure for a SATA SSD, ensuring its durability and longevity, particularly given its competitive price point. The use of 3D TLC NAND strikes a respectable balance between performance and durability, making it a dependable long-term choice.

The design of the Crucial MX500 in its 2.5-inch form factor is visually appealing, with a silver body and blue accents that add a touch of style to the drive. This design allows users to showcase the drive in their setups, enhancing its overall aesthetic appeal.

The MX500 runs away with the prize when it comes to the value discussion. SATA SSDs are known for their affordability, and the MX500 delivers superb value, offering an excellent price per gigabyte ratio. It is an ideal choice for gamers looking for cost-effective mass storage for games and large files in their gaming PCs. Combining a larger SATA drive like the MX500 with a smaller NVMe drive can allow gamers to achieve the best of both worlds without breaking the bank.

Choosing the best SSD for your needs

Selecting the best SSD for your particular needs can be a bit of a challenge. There are so many factors that can sway your decision one way or another depending on your preferences. However, if you want the best SSD for gaming, we feel pretty confident with our Editor’s Choice, the Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus.

The Rocket 4 Plus delivers exceptional overall performance, coupled with solid long-term reliability, and a stylish design. However, If you prioritize high performance and are willing to invest a bit more, the Samsung 990 PRO is also a compelling choice. You can even consider the blistering fast Corsair T700 PCIe 5.0 M.2 drive if you are feeling a bit brave.

Source: Sabrent
Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus 4TB SSD
$350 $1000 Save $650

The Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus, with its exceptional PCIe 4.0 technology, outstanding performance, and impressive 4TB capacity, is the top choice for premium gaming PCs.

$350 at Amazon

On the other end of the spectrum, we particularly like the XPG GAMMIX S70 BLADE, as it is certainly one of the best M.2 SSDs on a budget. The Crucial MX500 is a great wildcard pick for those gamers who just want to increase the storage capacity of their gaming rigs without breaking the bank.

Although it is easy to get confused when building a new PC for the first time, it is hard to go wrong with your SSD choice if you go with these curated picks. Choosing an SSD has become easier than ever thanks to the wealth of great options found on the market.
item.content:encoded:
As we create and consume content at an unprecedented pace, the need for substantial storage capacity, coupled with rapid data access, has become paramount. This is where the best 4TB SSDs step into the spotlight, offering a harmonious union of generous storage space and remarkable speed.

We've scoured the market to present you with a unique selection of the best SSDs available in a 4TB capacity today. Whether you're a creative professional who needs to store a huge backlog of assets, an enthusiast craving blazing speeds, or a gamer looking to load your favorite titles in the blink of an eye, our guide has you covered.

  • Source: Sabrent
    Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus 4TB SSD
    Editor's choice
    $350 at Amazon
  • Source: Samsung
    Samsung 990 PRO SSD 4TB
    Best performance
    $280 at Amazon
  • Source: Crucial
    Crucial T700 4TB SSD
    Premium pick
    $480 at Amazon
  • Source: XPG
    XPG GAMMIX S70 BLADE 4TB SSD
    Best value
    $263 at Amazon
  • Source: Corsair
    Corsair MP600 PRO LPX 4TB SSD
    Best SSD for PS5
    $280 at Amazon
  • Source: Teamgroup
    TEAMGROUP MP44 4TB SSD
    Best DRAM-less SSD
    $232 at Amazon
  • Source: Crucial
    Crucial P3 Plus 4TB SSD
    Best budget SSD
    $215 at Amazon
  • Source: Crucial
    Crucial MX500 4TB SSD
    Best SATA SSD
    $216 at Amazon
Source: Sabrent
Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus 4TB SSD
Editor's choice
$350 $1000 Save $650

The Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus is our Editor's Choice for the best 4 TB SSD, known for its exceptional performance, high reliability, and stylish design. However, its premium pricing might not suit budget-conscious buyers.

Pros
  • Excellent sequential performance
  • Solid long-term reliability
  • Even has an 8TB option
Cons
  • Relatively higher price
$350 at Amazon

The Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus earns our Editor's Choice for the best 4 TB SSD, setting the bar high thanks to its PCIe 4.0 storage technology. Its exceptional performance, reliability, and respectable value make it an ideal choice, particularly for premium gaming PCs.

Performance is where the Rocket 4 Plus truly shines. With a blazing sequential read speed of 7000 MB/s and a write speed of 6850 MB/s, it maxes out the capabilities of the PCIe 4.0 protocol. The SSD features a PCIe Gen 4×4 interface, a Phison PS50180-E18 flash controller, and an external DDR4 cache, improving its sustained performance. The 3D TLC NAND provides a perfect balance of speed and reliability.

On the topic of reliability, the 4TB Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus impresses here with a 3000 TBW rating, making it one of the most durable premium SSDs available. The use of Micron B27B TLC NAND Flash, along with advanced wear leveling and error correction code features, further solidifies its dependability.

Sabrent's attention to design is evident with the sleek rose-gold aesthetic, setting it apart from other SSDs. The optional massive heatsink not only adds to its visual appeal but also ensures optimal thermal performance should the user choose that option.

While the Rocket 4 Plus falls on the premium side of pricing, the value for money is still admirable. The upfront investment is justified by its exceptional performance and durability. Over time, this SSD proves its worth and becomes a justifiable choice for those seeking top-tier storage performance for their high-end PCs.

Source: Samsung
Samsung 990 PRO SSD 4TB
Best performance
$280 $345 Save $65

The Samsung 990 PRO is renowned for its extraordinary performance, delivering sequential speeds of 7450 MB/s Read and 6900 MB/s Write, paired with exceptional reliability, making it the top choice for high-performance storage enthusiasts and gamers.

Pros
  • Outstanding sustained speeds
  • Excellent optional heatsink
  • Various capacity options
Cons
  • Quite high price
$280 at Amazon

The Samsung 990 PRO represents the epitome of performance in the world of SSDs, particularly for those who demand the best. It harnesses the power of PCIe 4.0 NVMe technology, offering astonishing sequential Read speeds of 7450 MB/s and Write speeds of 6900 MB/s in its 4TB variant.

This impressive performance is achieved through the integration of V-NAND 3-bit MLC flash technology, a custom controller, and a DRAM cache, resulting in swift data access and multitasking with 1600K/1550K IOPS. It's the ideal choice for gamers and enthusiasts seeking top-tier storage performance.

Not only is the 990 PRO known for its exceptional performance, but it also excels in reliability. With a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) rating of 1.5 million hours and a 2400 TBW endurance rating for the 4TB variant, it exhibits exceptional long-term durability. The SSD incorporates TRIM and S.M.A.R.T features, further ensuring its dependability. Its Auto Garbage Collection Algorithm helps maintain optimal performance over time, making it a trustworthy storage solution.

The Samsung 990 PRO isn't just a powerhouse under the hood; it also boasts an elegant black design with subtle red accents. Users can even opt for a heatsink model, enhancing thermal efficiency and visual appeal. While it may come at a higher price, the SSD's exceptional performance makes it a compelling choice for enthusiasts and professionals who prioritize top-tier storage options. Its remarkable speed, durability, and dependability justify the premium price, making it an invaluable asset for enthusiasts and mainstream gamers alike.

Source: Crucial
Crucial T700 4TB SSD
Premium pick
$480 $600 Save $120

The Crucial T700 PCIe 5.0 NVMe SSD is the fastest consumer SSD out right now, offering groundbreaking performance with sequential read speeds of 12,400 MB/s thanks to its use of the PCIe 5.0 platform, though it comes at a premium price.

Pros
  • Fastest consumer SSD
  • Excellent reliability ratings
  • Well-made heatsink
Cons
  • Relatively more expensive
  • High thermal output
$480 at Amazon

For our premium pick, we chose the Crucial T700 PCIe 5.0 NVMe SSD thanks to its cutting-edge PCIe 5.0 platform, delivering groundbreaking performance for those seeking the pinnacle of storage technology.

The Crucial T700 truly redefines the limits of performance in the storage industry. Utilizing the new PCIe 5.0 standard, it achieves speeds of a staggering 12,400 MB/s for sequential read and 11,800 MB/s for write operations, making it the fastest consumer SSD in the world. These speeds far surpass PCIe 4.0 drives, providing a remarkable leap in data transfer capabilities. Whether for demanding gaming, content creation, or professional applications, the Crucial T700 sets a new performance benchmark.

Reliability is equally respectable, with a robust 2400 TBW (TeraBytes Written) endurance rating, ensuring the SSD's longevity. With a large capacity like 4TB, a 2400 TBW rating might seem a bit conservative, but a 5-year limited warranty further underscores Crucial’s confidence in its durability.

The design of the Crucial T700 is quite simple and minimalist, which is synonymous with its performance-focused nature. However, to manage the significant heat generated by a PCIe 5.0 SSD, an optional heatsink is also available. While this may be essential for efficient cooling, those who have effective M.2 heatsinks on their motherboards can save a few bucks here.

The Crucial T700's premium nature is evident, as it resides on the cutting edge of technology. However, the price tag is not entirely unreasonable, considering the groundbreaking performance it offers. Enthusiasts and tech aficionados seeking the latest and greatest in storage technology, regardless of cost, will find the Crucial T700's unique position appealing.

Source: XPG
XPG GAMMIX S70 BLADE 4TB SSD
Best value
$263 $290 Save $27

The XPG GAMMIX S70 Blade is a value-oriented SSD optimized for affordability and performance, making it an ideal choice for budget-conscious consumers seeking top-tier storage without the premium price tag.

Pros
  • Solid value for money
  • Excellent overall performance
  • Optimized for PS5
Cons
  • Lack of heatsink
$263 at Amazon

The XPG GAMMIX S70 Blade stands out as the best value SSD, offering impressive performance and reliability at an affordable price point.

Performance-wise, this SSD features the Micron 176-layer TLC V-NAND flash and can achieve speeds of up to 7400 MB/s read and 6800 MB/s write. Apart from amazing sequential read and write speeds, the drive also provides exceptional read-and-write IOPS of up to 750,000. Equipped with the Innogrit IG5236 controller, it competes with SSDs that bear a much higher price tag. Dynamic SLC Caching technology and a DRAM cache buffer further boost overall performance.

In terms of reliability, the XPG GAMMIX S70 Blade holds its own with an endurance rating of 2960 TBW and an MTBF rating of 2 million hours. It also features E2E Data Protection, a RAID engine, and AES 256-bit encryption, enhancing the drive's longevity and security. Moreover, the design, while not groundbreaking, features an all-black finish with an included sleek aluminum strip that serves as a heatsink, both functional and visually appealing.

What makes the XPG GAMMIX S70 Blade truly outstanding is its excellent value for money. It combines fast read and write speeds with high reliability, all at an affordable price point. It offers the performance and features of a premium SSD without the premium price tag, making it an ideal choice for budget-conscious consumers who want top-notch storage performance without breaking the bank. The XPG GAMMIX S70 Blade is the perfect solution for those looking for a cost-effective yet high-performing 4TB SSD.

Source: Corsair
Corsair MP600 PRO LPX 4TB SSD
Best SSD for PS5

The Corsair MP600 PRO LPX is the ideal SSD for both PC and PS5 use, delivering outstanding performance with read speeds of 7100 MB/s and excellent reliability with a 3000 TBW rating, all while maintaining optimal temperatures through an efficient heatsink.

Pros
  • Ideal for PS5 use
  • Exceptional sequential speeds
  • Outstanding heatsink performance
Cons
  • Tall heatsink might cause compatibility issues
$280 at Amazon

The Corsair MP600 PRO LPX is our pick for the best PS5 SSD, due to its outstanding performance regardless of whether you use it in a PC or a PS5.

Speaking of performance, the MP600 PRO LPX shines in this department. With a Phison E18 controller, Micron 176 Layer B47R NAND, and DRAM Cache, it delivers impressive sequential speeds of 7100 MB/s read, and 6800 MB/s write, while also boasting 1000K/1200K IOPS. These performance metrics ensure a smooth and rapid gaming experience on the PS5.

Reliability is a strong suit of the Corsair MP600 PRO LPX, featuring a TBW (TeraBytes Written) rating of 3000 for the 4TB model, indicating long-lasting durability. The MTBF (Mean Time Between Failures) of 1.8 million hours further adds to its reputation for reliability. Additionally, the SSD offers hardware encryption and comes with a five-year warranty, providing peace of mind to users.

The design of this SSD is tailored for the PS5, featuring a compact form factor with an efficient heatsink to maintain optimal temperatures during extended gaming sessions. The matte-black coating adds a sleek look to the drive, and the heatsink is easily removable, making it practical for those who may need to upgrade or maintain the SSD.

The Corsair MP600 PRO LPX shines when it comes to value for money. It manages to combine impressive performance, admirable reliability, and PS5 compatibility without going overboard on pricing. In a market often plagued by overpriced products, this SSD offers a refreshing balance of features and affordability.

Source: Teamgroup
TEAMGROUP MP44 4TB SSD
Best DRAM-less SSD

The TeamGroup MP44 4TB NVMe SSD delivers exceptional value for budget-conscious users, offering, solid reliability, and a variety of capacity options. While it lacks a DRAM cache, its strong price-to-performance ratio makes it a compelling choice for those seeking an affordable M.2 SSD.

Pros
  • Relatively low price
  • Solid reliability ratings
  • Excellent sequential speeds
Cons
  • Lack of DRAM cache
$232 at Amazon

The TeamGroup MP44 4TB NVMe SSD is an excellent value option for those willing to make a compromise. It offers impressive sequential performance, reliability, and exceptional value for money while sacrificing a bit of sustained performance due to the lack of a DRAM cache.

In terms of performance, the MP44 demonstrates surprisingly solid all-around capabilities, featuring impressive sequential read speeds of 7400 MB/s and write speeds of 6900 MB/s on the 4TB model. While it lacks a dedicated DRAM cache, the drive compensates with effective SLC caching, ensuring acceptable sustained performance. With outstanding IOPS of 650K for read and 660K for write operations, it's well-suited for tasks like gaming and even data-intensive applications.

Reliability is another strong suit of the TeamGroup MP44, with a Mean Time Between Failures (MTBF) of 1.6 million hours, showcasing its long-term durability. The 4TB variant boasts a Total Bytes Written (TBW) rating of 3000 TBW, making it capable of handling extensive data writing over its lifespan. The design of the MP44 is minimalist and simple, featuring a black-and-white color scheme that adds purpose to its utilitarian look. However, it lacks a heatsink, which could have helped with the thermal demands of the SSD.

Where the TeamGroup MP44 truly excels is when you consider its value for money. It offers a range of capacities to suit various storage needs while remaining budget-friendly. The combination of affordability and admirable performance makes it a standout choice with a positive price-to-performance ratio. If you're looking to maximize storage performance on a budget, the TeamGroup MP44 solidifies its position as one of the best DRAM-less SSD options in the market.

Source: Crucial
Crucial P3 Plus 4TB SSD
Best budget SSD

The Crucial P3 Plus is the ultimate budget SSD, combining solid performance and a very affordable price point, albeit with a slightly lower endurance rating. It's an excellent choice for price-conscious users seeking good value for their storage needs, backed by a 5-year warranty.

Pros
  • Outstanding value proposition
  • 5-year warranty
  • Solid sequential performance
Cons
  • Lower endurance rating
$215 at Amazon

The Crucial P3 Plus stands out as the best budget SSD, offering a respectable price-to-performance ratio at a very modest price point.

Performance is a strong suit of the Crucial P3 Plus, providing PCIe Gen 4.0 x4 speeds at a budget-friendly price point.

It utilizes Micron's 176-layer QLC NAND and the Phison PS5021-E2IT controller, resulting in respectable sequential read and write speeds of up to 4800 MB/s and 4100 MB/s, respectively. These speeds are competitive and suitable for a wide range of applications, including gaming and everyday tasks.

In terms of reliability, the 800TBW (TeraBytes Written) rating may not be the highest in its class, but Crucial's choice of high-quality NAND flash and a 5-year warranty offers peace of mind, ensuring the drive's longevity and dependability for several years to come.

The design of the Crucial P3 Plus is minimalist and straightforward, in line with its budget-oriented nature. It features a plain matte-black design with stickers covering the controller and memory cells. However, it lacks an onboard heatsink, which may be a drawback for users seeking better thermal performance, especially in prolonged usage scenarios.

The Crucial P3 Plus really stands out from the crowd when you consider its value proposition. It offers capacities up to 4TB, all at a lower price point compared to other PCIe Gen 4.0 drives. While the TBW rating is not the highest, it remains a cost-effective SSD solution for individuals on a tight budget, offering a balance of performance and affordability.

Source: Crucial
Crucial MX500 4TB SSD
Best SATA SSD
$216 $220 Save $4

The Crucial MX500 is a highly economical SATA SSD that offers good performance, durability, and a range of capacities, making it ideal for gamers who are looking to increase the storage capacity of their PCs. While it may not match NVMe speeds, it excels within its SATA limitations.

Pros
  • Very economical pricing
  • Great for mass storage
  • Various available capacities
Cons
  • Lower speeds than NVMe
$216 at Amazon

The Crucial MX500 excels within the limitations of its SATA 6Gbps interface. While it may not match the speeds of NVMe drives, it remains one of the fastest SATA SSDs available. With sequential read speeds of 560 MB/s and write speeds of 510 MB/s on the 4TB model, it delivers acceptable performance. Its random read/write rating of 95K/90K IOPS ensures sustained responsiveness, making it ideal for the mass storage of games and large files in gaming PCs.

The Crucial MX500 is also quite reliable, boasting an endurance rating of 1000 TBW for the 4TB model. This is an impressive figure for a SATA SSD, ensuring its durability and longevity, particularly given its competitive price point. The use of 3D TLC NAND strikes a respectable balance between performance and durability, making it a dependable long-term choice.

The design of the Crucial MX500 in its 2.5-inch form factor is visually appealing, with a silver body and blue accents that add a touch of style to the drive. This design allows users to showcase the drive in their setups, enhancing its overall aesthetic appeal.

The MX500 runs away with the prize when it comes to the value discussion. SATA SSDs are known for their affordability, and the MX500 delivers superb value, offering an excellent price per gigabyte ratio. It is an ideal choice for gamers looking for cost-effective mass storage for games and large files in their gaming PCs. Combining a larger SATA drive like the MX500 with a smaller NVMe drive can allow gamers to achieve the best of both worlds without breaking the bank.

Selecting the best SSD for your particular needs can be a bit of a challenge. There are so many factors that can sway your decision one way or another depending on your preferences. However, if you want the best SSD for gaming, we feel pretty confident with our Editor’s Choice, the Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus.

The Rocket 4 Plus delivers exceptional overall performance, coupled with solid long-term reliability, and a stylish design. However, If you prioritize high performance and are willing to invest a bit more, the Samsung 990 PRO is also a compelling choice. You can even consider the blistering fast Corsair T700 PCIe 5.0 M.2 drive if you are feeling a bit brave.

Source: Sabrent
Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus 4TB SSD
$350 $1000 Save $650

The Sabrent Rocket 4 Plus, with its exceptional PCIe 4.0 technology, outstanding performance, and impressive 4TB capacity, is the top choice for premium gaming PCs.

$350 at Amazon

On the other end of the spectrum, we particularly like the XPG GAMMIX S70 BLADE, as it is certainly one of the best M.2 SSDs on a budget. The Crucial MX500 is a great wildcard pick for those gamers who just want to increase the storage capacity of their gaming rigs without breaking the bank.

item.title: Massive price drop knocks 50% off this Blink outdoor wireless camera 3-pack bundle
item.itunes:title: Massive price drop knocks 50% off this Blink outdoor wireless camera 3-pack bundle
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Blink Outdoor (3rd Gen) 3 camera system
$125 $250 Save $125

A fantastic way to keep your home or office secure. This Blink wireless three-camera bundle is easy to install and is now 50% off for a limited time. My Best Buy members can save an additional $25 off the price, making it an even better deal.

$125 at Best Buy

If you're looking for a great way to monitor your home or office, this three-camera bundle from Blink is going to be an excellent option. It's easy to set up, can cover a large span of space and is now quite affordable at a price of just $125 for a limited time. My Best Buy members will be able to save just a touch more with an extra $25 off the already reduced price, bringing the three-camera bundle down to just $100.

As far as what you get, the Blink system comes with three outdoor wireless cameras that can shoot up to 1080p video and last up to two years, being powered by two AA lithium batteries. The cameras are protected from the elements, which means you can feel confident leaving them outside even on the hottest days or stormy nights.

In addition to the three cameras, the system also includes the Blink Sync Module 2, which allows images and videos captured by the cameras to be saved locally to a memory card. You can connect up to ten cameras to this system, which makes expanding easy. The company does offer cloud storage as well, but this will require a monthly service subscription.

For the most part, you can't go wrong with this Blink camera system, offering a lot of features and coming in at a relatively affordable price. Also, since it's the holiday season, you're going to get an extended return period until January 13, 2024. So make sure to grab this deal while you can.
